meta.name: GraphQL Attacks
meta.description: GraphQL attack payloads covering introspection abuse, injection, DoS via nested queries, SSRF, authorization bypass, and batching attacks.

payloads.description: Full schema introspection — dumps all type names; first step in recon

payloads.code: { __schema { queryType { name } mutationType { name } subscriptionType { name } types { name kind description fields { name type { name kind ofType { name kind } } } } } }
payloads.description: Deep schema dump — full introspection including all fields, types, and relationships

payloads.code: { __type(name: "User") { name fields { name type { name kind } } } }
payloads.description: Type introspection — dump all fields of a specific type (replace User with target type)
Fragment-based introspection — some WAFs block __schema but allow fragment introspection
Introspection with comment suffix — bypass filters that check exact query endings
Direct data dump — query sensitive fields directly; test what fields are accessible without auth
IDOR via GraphQL — access another user's data by changing the ID argument
Unauthorized mutation — attempt to modify another user's role via mutation
SQLi via GraphQL argument — inject SQL into GraphQL resolver arguments
Stacked query injection via GraphQL — pass destructive SQL through argument
XSS via GraphQL — inject script tags into search/text arguments if reflected unsanitized
SSTI via GraphQL argument — template injection through query arguments

payloads.code: { a: user(id:1){email} b: user(id:2){email} c: user(id:3){email} d: user(id:4){email} e: user(id:5){email} }
payloads.description: Query aliasing — use GraphQL aliases to enumerate multiple IDs in a single request
Batched query array — send multiple operations in one HTTP request to bypass rate limits

payloads.code: { a1: user{a2: user{a3: user{a4: user{a5: user{email}}}}}}
payloads.description: Deeply nested query — exponential resolver complexity causes CPU/memory DoS

payloads.code: query { users { friends { friends { friends { friends { id email } } } } } }
payloads.description: Circular relationship DoS — exploit circular references in schema to exhaust server resources
SSRF via GraphQL mutation — trick server into fetching internal URLs via data import resolver
SSRF via webhook/callback argument — any URL-accepting argument can be leveraged for SSRF
Comment injection in mutation argument — terminate SQL query inside resolver with inline comment
Minimal introspection probe — __typename works even when full introspection is disabled; confirms GraphQL endpoint
Named introspection query — some WAFs only block unnamed introspection; add query name to bypass
Hasura/Prisma filter injection — ORM-style filters may allow unauthorized field access
